text.tspan: Traditional tool-using agent: emit one tool call -> wait -> parse result -> emit next.
N steps = N round-trips, lots of intermediate tokens, slow.
Code Mode: emit ONE PROGRAM that orchestrates ALL the tool calls, run it, return result.
contents = {f: read_file(f) for f in files}
Result returned in ONE LLM call.
2. Code mode wraps each tool as a function in the
3. Agent writes code that calls the wrapped functions.
4. Code runs in sandboxed runtime (E2B, Modal, Pyodide).
5. Output captured and returned to agent.
+ Loops, conditionals, error handling in code
+ Easier audit (the code is the plan)
